title: Renaissance Art in Florence
extract: Florence served as the cradle of the Renaissance, a cultural movement that fundamentally transformed European art, architecture, and thought from the 14th to the 17th century. The Medici family's patronage created an unprecedented environment for artistic innovation, nurturing masters like Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Botticelli. Walking through the Uffizi Gallery today, you can still feel the revolutionary energy that drove these artists to break from medieval conventions. The city's churches and palazzos remain living testaments to an era when art was not merely decoration but a profound exploration of human potential.
